content.full_text:
Date: 1:08 PM 52
20794 RUSSELL LANE
SARATOGA, CA 95070

December 3, 1992

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

ID# 922620

Dear Sir:

I was referred to you by the Political Reform Division of
the Secretary of State's office. I am the treasurer of the above
numbered committee to oppose a Santa Clara ballot measure in the
election held November 1992. We did not collect enough money to
qualify as a recipient committee under the Political Reform Act.

We are considering a lawsuit against what we feel is an
illegal tax. My question to the above mentioned office was: if we
collect the many thousands expected to support such a lawsuit,
does that money get reported to them under our previously
established committee. The answer I was given was to the effect
that since the measure was voted on and the election over, it
would not be correct to use their system to report future
collections or expenditures for a lawsuit.

They did, however, suggest that I write you for your opinion
in this matter. I would appreciate your comments on the
foregoing.

Sincerely yours,

[signature]
George A. Kreamer

[The image appears to be mostly blank/white with very faint text that is not clearly legible. There are some horizontal lines visible but the text content is too faint to transcribe with confidence.]
